title: The Systems Descent
description: Trace the path from high-level managed code down to the metal, and finally to modern memory safety.

steps.rationale: Understand the comfort of Garbage Collection and the JVM before losing the safety net.
steps.challenge: Identify the overhead introduced by the JVM's memory management.

steps.rationale: Experience the power and danger of manual memory management and pointers.
steps.challenge: Implement a linked list and manually manage every byte without leaking.

steps.rationale: Discover how the Borrow Checker provides C-level performance with Java-level safety.
steps.challenge: Refactor a C-style pointer implementation to pass the Rust borrow checker.

title: The Concurrency Evolution
description: Explore how different languages handle the challenge of doing many things at once.

steps.rationale: Learn the traditional shared-memory model with mutexes and locks.
steps.challenge: Resolve a classic deadlock scenario in a multi-threaded application.

steps.rationale: Shift from shared memory to 'communicating by sharing' via channels.
steps.challenge: Implement a worker pool using goroutines and channels for synchronization.

steps.rationale: Experience the ultimate isolation: no shared state, only asynchronous message passing.
steps.challenge: Build a fault-tolerant supervisor tree that restarts failing processes.

title: The Functional Shift
description: Move away from imperative instructions towards declarative expressions and pure functions.

steps.rationale: Return to the roots of functional programming where code is data.
steps.challenge: Write a macro that extends the language with a new control structure.

steps.rationale: Enforce side-effect freedom with a rigorous, lazy type system.
steps.challenge: Use Monads to handle IO while maintaining functional purity.

steps.rationale: See how functional principles scale to massive distributed systems.
steps.challenge: Scale a functional web service to handle 10,000 concurrent connections.

title: The Web's Architectural Journey
description: Follow the web's transition from simple scripts to robust, type-safe engineering.

steps.rationale: Understand the birth of the dynamic web and the request-response cycle.
steps.challenge: Create a session-managed application using only core language features.

steps.rationale: Bring interactivity to the browser and learn the event loop.
steps.challenge: Implement an asynchronous data fetcher that handles race conditions.

steps.rationale: Add the safety of a modern type system to the web's most flexible language.
steps.challenge: Convert a dynamic JS library to a strictly typed TS module.

title: FinTech Performance & Safety
description: A focused project on building high-frequency trading systems where every microsecond and every bit of safety counts.

steps.rationale: Used for the absolute fastest execution paths in trading engines.
steps.challenge: Optimize an order matching algorithm for sub-microsecond latency.

steps.rationale: Handles the complex business logic and reporting with high throughput.
steps.challenge: Tune the G1 garbage collector to minimize 'stop-the-world' pauses.

steps.rationale: The emerging choice for combining C++ speed with absolute memory safety.
steps.challenge: Rewrite a critical risk-check module in Rust to eliminate segfault risks.

title: The Space-Grade Constraint
description: Develop software for environments where failure is not an option and resources are extremely limited.

steps.rationale: The gold standard for safety-critical systems since the 1980s.
steps.challenge: Use Ravenscar profile to ensure deterministic real-time behavior.

steps.rationale: The lingua franca of hardware interfacing in space missions.
steps.challenge: Write a hardware driver for a radiation-hardened processor.

steps.rationale: A fresh take on systems programming with better safety than C but same control.
steps.challenge: Use Zig's comptime to generate optimized telemetry packets.
